name: Pages with Sections, Hidden Tasks, and Task List Options
summary: Advanced pages layout demonstrating section groupings, hidden task list items, additional instructions, and customized review flow — the full Design System basic layout pattern
whenToUse: Large government application forms with distinct logical sections
When you want a task-list view with section groupings and progress tracking
When some steps should be hidden from the Task List (e.g. sub-steps rolled into a parent)
When you need to customize the review flow (hide summary, hide submit, change labels)

options.title: Application Form
options.subtitle: Tell us about yourself
options.additionalInstructions: Complete all required sections before submitting your application.

notes: Use `options.variant: "pages"` for the Design System's preferred pages pattern
Each Category becomes a page; `sectionTitle` groups categories into sections on the task list
Categories sharing the same `sectionTitle` are grouped under one section heading
Set `showInTaskList: false` on a Category to hide it from the Task List — the page still renders but the row is hidden and completion is rolled into the previous visible task in the same section
For nested objects, scopes must use the full path: `#/properties/name/properties/firstName`
A Summary page is automatically appended by the GoA renderer for applicant review
Use `options.title` and `options.subtitle` on Categorization for the task list header
Use `toAppOverviewLabel` to customize the back link text (default: 'Back to application overview')
CRITICAL: Category elements can ONLY be direct first-level children of the Categorization `elements` array — do not nest them inside other layouts
Use `additionalInstructions` to display guidance text on the Task List page
Set `hideSummary: true` to remove the Summary row and skip the review page entirely
Set `hideSubmit: true` to keep the review page but hide the Submit button
Set `hideProgress: true` to hide the completion indicators on the Task List
